ProxyGroup.name: filters
ProxyGroup.SourceProxy:
TTK filter for generic dimension reduction methods. This filter supports various methods via the scikit-learn third party dependency (spectral embedding, local linear embedding, multi-dimensional scaling, t-SNE, isomap, PCA) as well as topology-aware methods, e.g. TopoMap (IEEE VIS 2020), TopoAE++ (2025).
Online examples:
- https://topology-tool-kit.github.io/examples/1manifoldLearning/
- https://topology-tool-kit.github.io/examples/clusteringKelvinHelmholtzInstabilities/
- https://topology-tool-kit.github.io/examples/karhunenLoveDigits64Dimensions/
- https://topology-tool-kit.github.io/examples/mergeTreeClustering/
- https://topology-tool-kit.github.io/examples/mergeTreePGA/
- https://topology-tool-kit.github.io/examples/persistenceDiagramPGA/
- https://topology-tool-kit.github.io/examples/persistentGenerators_householdAnalysis/
- https://topology-tool-kit.github.io/examples/persistentGenerators_periodicPicture/
- https://topology-tool-kit.github.io/examples/topoAEppTeaser/
- https://topology-tool-kit.github.io/examples/topoMapTeaser/
Related publication:
"Topomap: A 0-dimensional homology preserving projection of high-dimensional data". H.Doraiswamy, J. Tierny, P. J. S. Silva, L. G. Nonato and C. Silva. IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics 27(2): 561-571, 2020.
"Topological Autoencoders" Michael Moor, Max Horn, Bastian Rieck, Karsten Borgwardt, 37th International Conference on Machine Learning, 2020.
"Optimizing persistent homology-based functions" Mathieu Carriere, Frederic Chazal, Marc Glisse, Yuichi Ike, Hariprasad Kannan, Yuhei Umeda, 38th International Conference on Machine Learning, 2021.
"Topological Autoencoders++: Fast and Accurate Cycle-Aware Dimensionality Reduction" MattÃ©o ClÃ©mot, Julie Digne, Julien Tierny, IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics. Accepted, to be presented at IEEE VIS 2026.
Data-set to process.
Select input scalar fields matching a regular expression.
Select the scalar fields to process.
This regexp will be used to filter the chosen fields. Only matching ones will be selected.
Set the number of output components (i.e. dimensions).
Set the number of neighbors.
Keep all data arrays.
The Spectral Embedding and MDS methods can be fed directly with dissimilarity (distance) matrices instead of raw point clouds.
Check this box if the input represents an image dataset. An autoencoder with convolutional layers will be used instead of an autoencoder with dense layers only. The images are supposed to be square.
Documentation.
When projecting in 2D, we regularly need to merge two already projected subsets of the points. We put them apart the right distance, but we may rotate each by some angle between two bounds. We test several angles and keep the best one. This parameter defines how many tests we do for each rotation. Beware, this parameter appears squared in the algorithm complexity.
This way to embed points in 2D is based on preserving the lengths of the edges selected by Kruskal Algorithm to build a spanning tree. Checking this box enables testing that these lengths are indeed preserved. This test is very quick.
Coefficient applied to the chosen topological regularization term during the optimization.
Batch size used during the optimization. Put 0 to use the whole dataset at each iteration.
Size of the hidden layers in the encoder network. The number of integers controls the number of hidden layers. The decoder network will have the same number of hidden layers with sizes in the reverse order. In case of convolutional layers, use the syntax c{#output channels}/{stride}: for example c8/2 creates a convolutional layer with an 8-channels, half-resolution output.
Check this to make the behavior of LibTorch as deterministic as possible (deterministic algorithms, fixed seeds), for example for reproducibility purpose. In this case, CUDA is disabled and the CPU will be used instead.
Set the seed for randomness.
Check this to initialize the autoencoder on the output of another dimension reduction method.
Select input scalar fields matching a regular expression.
Select the initialization fields.
This regexp will be used to filter the chosen fields for initialization. Only matching ones will be selected.
Use CUDA if available. If no CUDA device is available, or this is not checked, the CPU will be used instead.
Set the path of the Python module.
Set the name of the Python module.
Set the name of the Python function.
Set the random state.
${DEBUG_WIDGETS}
